steps.title: Identify what drives adoption
steps.content: The researchers found that performance expectancy — the perceived benefit of the service — positively influences the intention to adopt premium versions. This is the key signal: perceived benefit directly increases adoption intention.

steps.title: Apply the signal to each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — users' intentions to pay for premium steaming services are probably unaffected by factors other than performance expectancy.: Overstates the research. The study showed performance expectancy positively influences adoption intention, not that adoption is unaffected by all other factors. ✗
  • Choice B — participants who strongly agreed with the statement "Overall, a paid music streaming service is advantageous" were more likely to express loyalty to Spotify than to other streaming services.: Confuses the scope of the study. The research did not compare brand loyalty to Spotify versus other streaming services. ✗
  • Choice C — the statement "Overall, a paid music streaming service is advantageous" is not a reliable measure of performance expectancy.: Contradicts the study's own methodology. The researchers used user statements about perceived usefulness as a valid measure of performance expectancy — they treated those statements as meaningful, not suspect. ✗
  • Choice D — it may be advantageous for music streaming companies to try to influence the extent to which potential users perceive the use of the premium version to be beneficial.: Follows directly from the finding. If perceived benefit drives adoption intention, companies should try to enhance how beneficial users perceive the premium service. ✓
That is the correct answer.
